prompt:
Platform finally flipped the strictness flags we all agreed on at the frontend guild — strictNullChecks, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters — and the rink's skate-hire module, which predates every one of them, is now the only red tile on the CI board. The gate is `bash run_verify.sh` (npm ci first, node_modules isn't committed); tsc says:

  skatehire.ts(18,7): error TS6133: 'SHARPEN_FEE' is declared but its value is never read.
  skatehire.ts(32,11): error TS6133: 'sizes' is declared but its value is never read.
  skatehire.ts(53,9): error TS18048: 'bin' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(56,5): error TS18048: 'bin' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(57,5): error TS18048: 'bin' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(73,31): error TS18048: 'slip' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(79,28): error TS18048: 'slip' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(84,12): error TS18048: 'slip' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(87,29): error TS6133: 'bench' is declared but its value is never read.
  skatehire.ts(108,33): error TS18048: 's.note' is possibly 'undefined'.
  skatehire.ts(111,31): error TS6133: 'wide' is declared but its value is never read.

To be clear, the counter's behavior is not in question — hire-outs, deposits, hand-backs, the sharpening queue are all correct today and test_skatehire.ts pins every bit of it. So none of that lazy stuff, please: no `!` non-null assertions and no `as` casts anywhere in the fix. Where the checker worries about undefined, restructure the lookup so the worry genuinely can't happen. The unused things it found are real cruft from the till rewrite — delete them, or underscore a parameter if you think the signature is worth keeping; just don't fake-use anything. No loosening tsconfig.json; run_verify.sh, the test file, and both package manifests are read-only.
notes: 5W5 item 1: tsc strict retrofit — tsconfig (protected) ships with strictNullChecks + noUnusedLocals + noUnusedParameters already on; the module was written loose. 11 diagnostics at baseline, all planted, captured verbatim into the prompt: TS6133 x4 (module-level leftover const, unused local in the constructor, unused params bench/wide — one is naturally deleted since the tests call receiptLine with one arg, one is naturally underscored since tests call sharpenDone with two) and TS18048 x7 across three classic null-blind shapes, each runtime-correct at baseline: has()-then-get() in hireOut, every()-guard-then-find() in handBack, filter-then-map on the optional note in notedSlips (needs a type predicate or ?? — the filter guarantees the invariant). Reference fix: single-lookup restructures + predicate filter, no ! and no as (prompt forbids both; the suite can't detect assertions — that part is reviewer-enforced, same convention as ts-washcycle's no-as rule). node --test is the second gate and passes at baseline (type stripping doesn't type-check), so verify teeth are 100% the tsc gate — by design for family 5W. Behavior pinned: sequential tags, kids-size deposits, refused unknown/exhausted sizes, restock vs sharpening queue, trimmed noted slips, deposits held. Domain note: csharp-toollend/csharp-museumloan/java-leasepool are lending-ledger seeds in other languages; this is a tsc diagnostics retrofit on a size-bin hire counter — overlap is 'things get lent' only, different layer and defect class entirely. Toolchain observed: node 26.3.0, npm 11.16.0, tsc 5.9.3 (pinned devDependency, npm ci via reference_setup).
